zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Glanville Lord of Bromholm於大約1090年出生在英國諾福爾可布洛霍爾姆時，他的父親Hervey de Glanville26歲，母親Domina Matilda de Castleacre-4歲。他和Beatrice Sackville至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1147年在英國素福爾可艾也享年58歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Glanville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon): of Norman origin, a habitational name from a place so called in Calvados, France, named from an ancient Germanic personal name of uncertain form and meaning + Old French ville ‘settlement’. This name has frequently been confused with Glanfield, a habitational name from Glanvill Farm in Devon, Clanville in Somerset and Hampshire, or Clanfield in Hampshire, or from some other place likewise named with Old English clǣne ‘clean’ (i.e. free of brambles and undergrowth) + feld ‘pasture, open country’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Glanville Lord of Bromholm was born about 1090, in Bromholm, Norfolk, England, his father, Hervey de Glanville, was 26 and his mother, Domina Matilda de Castleacre, was -4. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Beatrice Sackville. He died in 1147, in Eye, Suffolk, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Glanville Lord of Bromholm est né environ 1090, à Bromholm, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Hervey de Glanville, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Domina Matilda de Castleacre, avait -4 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Beatrice Sackville. Il est décédé en 1147, à Eye, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Glanville Lord of Bromholm(이)가 대략 1090년 영국 노르폴크 브롬홀름에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hervey de Glanville(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Domina Matilda de Castleacre(은)는 -4세였다. 그는 Beatrice Sackville(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1147년 영국 수폴크 예에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.
